sections.title: Credits
sections.notes: Standard publication credits; useful only for meta reference, not RAG lore.

sections.title: Special Thanks
sections.notes: Acknowledgments and edition-specific thanks; can generally be ignored for in-game reference.

sections.title: Chapter One: Dusk in Marrakesh
sections.notes: Opening fiction establishing the mood, themes of mentorship, and the Lasombra's relationship with shadows and control.

sections.title: Chapter Two: An Evening's Discussion with Monsignor Alfonse
sections.anchor: ## CHAPTER TWO:AN EVENING's DISCUSSION WITH MONSIGNOR ALFONSE
sections.notes: Fiction section exploring Lasombra's relationship with the Church and religious institutions.

sections.title: Chapter Three: An Evening at the Feet of Don Miguel
sections.anchor: ## CHAPTER THREE:AN EVENING AT THE FEET OF DON MIGdEL
sections.notes: Fiction section presenting a lecture or teaching session about Lasombra philosophy and history.

sections.title: Tzimisce
sections.notes: Lasombra's view of the Tzimisce clan, their relationship, and strategic considerations.

sections.title: Assamite
sections.notes: Lasombra's perspective on the Assamites, viewing them as useful tools that must be handled with respect.

sections.title: Brujah
sections.notes: Lasombra's contemptuous view of the Brujah as failed egalitarians.

sections.title: Gangrel
sections.notes: Lasombra's dismissive view of the Gangrel as evolutionary failures.

sections.title: Giovanni
sections.notes: Lasombra's assessment of the Giovanni as potential threats with a fatal weakness.

sections.title: Malkavian
sections.notes: Lasombra's warning about the dangers of listening to Malkavians.

sections.title: Nosferatu
sections.notes: Lasombra's recognition of similarities with the Nosferatu while noting their limitations.

sections.title: Panders
sections.notes: Lasombra's contemptuous view of the Panders as mongrels.

sections.title: Ravnos
sections.notes: Lasombra's view of the Ravnos as tests of patience.

sections.title: Serpents of the Light
sections.notes: Lasombra's assessment of the Serpents as predictable and useful when directed.

sections.title: Toreador
sections.notes: Lasombra's view of the Toreador as victims of a fine distinction, useful for maintaining civilized appearances.

sections.title: Tremere
sections.notes: Lasombra's warning about the Tremere as dangerous sorcerers who must be kept under control.

sections.title: Ventrue
sections.notes: Lasombra's contempt for the Ventrue as weak imitations of true leadership.

sections.title: Black Hand
sections.notes: Lasombra's perspective on the Black Hand and how to control it.

sections.title: Camarilla
sections.notes: Lasombra's view of the Camarilla as a failed experiment that must be broken and rebuilt.

sections.title: Antitribu
sections.notes: Lasombra's dismissive view of Lasombra Antitribu as oddities.

sections.title: Lupines
sections.notes: Lasombra's strategic view of werewolves as dangerous but shortsighted enemies.

sections.title: Mages
sections.notes: Lasombra's assessment of mages as self-regulating threats.

sections.title: Wraiths
sections.notes: Lasombra's warning about the dangers of creating too many wraiths.

sections.title: Changelings
sections.notes: Lasombra's view of the fae as potentially useful but dangerous.

sections.title: The Rarest of Vintages
sections.notes: Discussion of diablerie within the clan and the Court of Blood system for regulating it.

sections.title: The Making of a Lasombra
sections.notes: Explanation of how Lasombra choose their childer and what qualities they seek.

sections.title: The Mentoring Period
sections.notes: Description of how Lasombra train and mentor their childer, emphasizing survival of the fittest.

sections.title: Les Amies Noir
sections.notes: Introduction to Les Amies Noir, the inner circle of trusted Lasombra who guide clan policy.

sections.title: Being an Account of the Chess Match Between Don Ibrahim and Dona Isadora
sections.anchor: ## BEING AN ACCOINT OFTHE CHESSMATCHBETWEENDON IBRAHIM AND DONA ISADORA
sections.notes: A detailed account of a chess match between two Lasombra, serving as both entertainment and a lesson in clan culture and politics.

sections.title: Chapter Four: Acceptable Modern Lasombra
sections.notes: Guidance on creating modern Lasombra characters, including archetypes, backgrounds, and roleplaying advice.

sections.title: Certain Especially Noteworthy Lasombra
sections.notes: Profiles of notable Lasombra characters, including their backgrounds, motivations, and potential plot hooks.
